question: What is the definition of the abbreviation PZR?

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates at full power using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It relies on passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not require electrical power, and its components are housed in steel containment partially immersed in water for effective heat removal. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its modular construction enables in-shop fabrication of major components.

nuscale_ch01_0769.title: SRP 15.6.1, Rev 2: Inadvertent Opening of a PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve or a BWR Pressure Relief ValveAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0769.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 15.6.1, as it does not use power-operated pressurizer relief valves (PORVs) that are susceptible to inadvertent opening; instead, it uses spring-loaded ASME code safety relief valves, which do not have the same vulnerability. Any mechanical failure of the reactor safety valve is considered to be bounded by the analysis of inadvertent ECCS valve actuation, as discussed in Section 15.6.6.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis report diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and operations, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and level indicators, ensuring standardized interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

edges.description: Section B provides diagram legends that may define abbreviations such as PZR, which could be referenced or used in Section A's discussion of operating characteristics.
Section B provides the legend for abbreviations and symbols used in diagrams, which likely includes the definition of 'PZR' referenced in Section A's context of pressurizer relief valves.

Section A provides general information about acronyms and abbreviations in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B references the use of the abbreviation 'PZR' in the context of regulatory criteria, connecting them through the use and definition of technical abbreviations.
